Petition For Order(S) And-Or Directive(S) To Financial Institutions And The San Francisco County Registrar
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gilbert Jay, via Zoom, for Elizabeth Cranke, Administrator.

The court advises that its tentative ruling is to deny this petition. Counsel offers advocacy on behalf of the petition specifically as to the request for an order to the Registrar of the City and County of San Francisco to provide the Administrator with a certified copy of Darlene McDonnell's death certificate and believes that the other requests in the petition can be resolved by the Administrator without a court order, by having the certified copy of the death certificate.

After additional discussion, the Petition is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Dec-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gilbert Jay for Elizabeth Cranke.

The Court stated that it was looking for the filing of the Inventory & Appraisal and the filing of the Petition for Final Distribution. Mr. Jay provided a brief background referencing the decedent's Mother's estate in case no. PES-05-287249 (the Estate of Henrietta Rosenblatt). Mr. Jay also stated that the Henrietta Rosenblatt case was a difficult one, that Robert Rosenblatt died in 2018, that they then filed the petition, that they are attempting to find assets, that they have found some accounts, that they received the notes, that they might have some assets, and that they are seeking an additional 60-day continuance to perform the necessary due diligence to marshall the assets and pay the creditors. The Court stated that this matter was already past the statutory deadline. The Court then continued the status on the filing of the Inventory & Appraisal and status on the filing of a Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration to December 4, 2019 at 9;00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: M. Nuque. Matter not reported.
